name: Fand
description: Fand is a prominent figure in Irish mythology, often depicted as a queen of the fairies or the Otherworld, specifically Tír na nÓg. Her name translates to 'pearl' or 'sacred woman,' reflecting her beauty and status. She is associated with love, beauty, healing, and magic. Fand is known for her ethereal beauty, described as having hair like spun gold and a radiant presence. She is a skilled warrior and possesses magical abilities. One of her most famous stories involves her love affair with the hero Cú Chulainn. She fell deeply in love with him and offered him her love and kingdom, but ultimately released him from his obligations after his wife, Emer, confronted her with love and loyalty. This tale showcases Fand's complex character, demonstrating both her power and her capacity for compassion. She is often seen as a powerful and independent figure, choosing her own path and acting according to her own desires. Her role in the mythology highlights the connection between the mortal and Otherworldly realms and the potential for both conflict and harmony between them. Fand's story is a testament to the enduring power of love, loyalty, and self-sacrifice.
subtitle: Queen of the Fairies, Beloved of Cú Chulainn

keyMyths.title: Fand and Cú Chulainn
keyMyths.description: Fand falls in love with Cú Chulainn and offers him her love and kingdom. However, he must choose between her and his wife, Emer.

keyMyths.title: Fand's Healing Powers
keyMyths.description: Fand is known for her ability to heal the sick and wounded. She uses her magic to restore balance and harmony.

keyMyths.title: Fand's Farewell
keyMyths.description: After Emer confronts Fand, Fand releases Cú Chulainn from his obligations, choosing to return to the Otherworld alone.

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
